claim_text: The Serco matter has direct regulator notice text identifying both the statutory notice posture and the cited UK GDPR contraventions.
verbatim_support_or_exact_paraphrase: The ICO enforcement notice to Serco Leisure Operating Limited states that the Commissioner issued the notice under Data Protection Act 2018 section 149(2)(a) and (c) in relation to contraventions of Articles 5(1)(a), 6, and 9 UK GDPR.
reviewer_caution: Use as direct-notice legal-basis support only; do not convert one notice into broader biometric-sector prevalence or judicial fact-finding.

claim_text: The Serco notice supports a narrow remedy claim that the ICO required cessation of biometric attendance processing and destruction of retained data not legally required to be kept.
verbatim_support_or_exact_paraphrase: Annex 1 requires Serco within three months to cease processing biometric data for employment attendance checks at relevant facilities and destroy biometric and other personal/special-category data not legally required to be retained.
reviewer_caution: Supports remedy-text comparison and document-depth tables, not downstream compliance completion.

claim_text: The Chelmer Valley High School matter has direct regulator reprimand text identifying the Article 35(1) DPIA failure and the deployment timing.
verbatim_support_or_exact_paraphrase: The ICO reprimand to Chelmer Valley High School states that the Commissioner issued a reprimand under Article 58(2)(b) UK GDPR in respect of Article 35(1), and records that the school introduced facial recognition for cashless catering in March 2023 without a prior DPIA.
reviewer_caution: Keep school context metadata-first and avoid unnecessary factual expansion beyond the reprimand text.

claim_text: The Chelmer reprimand supports a narrow claim that the school's assumed opt-out consent approach was not valid lawful consent for the biometric processing described in the reprimand.
verbatim_support_or_exact_paraphrase: The reprimand states that from March to November 2023 the controller relied on assumed opt-out consent that would not have been valid or lawful consent for the processing.
reviewer_caution: Supports process-failure and consent-framing discussion only; do not generalize to all school biometric deployments.

claim_text: Taken together, the two UK ICO document-grade rows support a bounded comparative claim that LIMEN can show distinct biometric-governance failure modes across workplace attendance monitoring and school canteen payments using direct regulator documents rather than summary-only pages.
verbatim_support_or_exact_paraphrase: Serco contributes direct notice text on cited UK GDPR provisions and Annex 1 cessation/destruction remedies, while Chelmer contributes direct reprimand text on Article 35(1), March 2023 deployment timing, and invalid opt-out consent framing.
reviewer_caution: Comparative methods/result use only; not evidence of sector-wide prevalence, harmonized liability, or broader UK biometric legality conclusions.
